entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A rainfed crop grown mainly on light soils in semiarid and arid regions, scientifically known as Pennisetum americanum (L.) Leeke

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A lack of water availability that reduces crop yields, often occurring when rains end early

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: A critical growth stage of the crop affected by the timing of water deficit

entities.name: GRAIN-FILLING
entities.description: A critical growth stage of the crop affected by the timing of water deficit

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research approach used to investigate the response of pearl millet to water deficit

entities.name: LINE-SOURCE SPRINKLER SYSTEM
entities.description: A system used to simulate varying intensity and timing of water deficit in the field

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: The specific soil type (Udic Rhodustalf) used in the experiments

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: A characteristic of genotypes that influences the timing of terminal stress

entities.name: TERMINAL STRESS
entities.description: Water deficit occurring at the end of the growth cycle

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced, which is reduced by water deficit intensity and timing

entities.name: GRAIN NUMBER
entities.description: The number of grains per unit area, reduced by water deficit intensity and affected by timing

entities.name: GRAIN SIZE
entities.description: The size of the grains, reduced by water deficit intensity but not by timing

entities.name: STRESS INTENSITY
entities.description: The degree or severity of the water deficit

entities.name: TIMING OF STRESS ONSET
entities.description: The point in time when water deficit begins relative to the flowering stage

entities.name: SEMIARID AND ARID REGIONS
entities.description: The geographical areas where pearl millet is primarily grown
